originalAlarm.description: Undefined or garbage value returned to caller

sanitizedDescription: Undefined or garbage value returned to caller
model:
(define-fun __int8_t_defined () Bool
  false)
(define-fun CONFIG_64BIT () Bool
  false)
(define-fun CONFIG_KTIME_SCALAR () Bool
  false)
reasoning: Both branches of the if-else at L38-43 assign tmp (L39: tmp=nsec; L42: tmp=ktime_set(...)), so return tmp at L45 is always reading an initialized value. The alarm is a false positive. The PC contains restrictive library macro !__int8_t_defined (stdint.h not yet processed), which causes the s32/s64 typedefs to be undefined in the desugared analysis, leading CSA to mistrack the type of tmp. The known VBDB bug (// ERROR at L42) is a distinct issue involving narrowing cast (s32)sec.
